name: Informationextractor Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Informationextractor Workflow. This workflow integrates 14 different services: stickyNote, code, agent, noOp, informationExtractor. It contains 22 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Information Extractor
nodes.notes: This informationExtractor node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: If Node
nodes.notes: This if node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Google Sheets
nodes.notes: This googleSheets node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Code

parameters.pythonCode:
# Input from n8n
input_data = items

# Get the extracted list
extract_data = input_data[0].get('json', {}).get('output', {}).get('parameters', {}).get('extract', [])

# Prepare variables
order_items = []
table_number = None

# Separate entries by type
items_list = []
quantities = []

# Parse all entries
for entry in extract_data:
    if entry['name'] == 'table number':
        table_number = entry['pattern']
    elif entry['name'] == 'item':
        items_list.append(entry['pattern'])
    elif entry['name'] == 'quantity':
        quantities.append(int(entry['pattern']))

# Pair items and quantities
for i in range(len(items_list)):
    item_data = {
        'item': items_list[i],
        'quantity': quantities[i] if i < len(quantities) else None,
        'table': table_number
    }
    order_items.append(item_data)

# Set final output
output = [{'json': item} for item in order_items]

return output

nodes.name: AI Agent

options.systemMessage:
You are a polite and efficient restaurant assistant.

Your job is to take customer orders, verify the order details, correct any mistakes, and confirm the order.

Follow these steps:

Greeting and Asking for the Order

If the customer greets you (e.g., "Hello", "Hi", "Good evening"), respond with:

"Hello! How can I assist you today? What would you like to order?"

Order Parsing and Understanding

Accept orders in flexible formats, such as:

"1 latte, 2 coffee, table number 5"

"latte 2, pepsi 1, table 3"

"1 cappucino"

"1 tea table no 4"

Your goal is to extract the following:

Item names (e.g., latte, coffee, chocolate, tea, pepsi)

Quantities (must be numeric)

Table number (must be numeric)

Verify and Handle Missing or Incorrect Information

For each item in the order:

If the item name is missing, respond:
"Sorry, the item name is missing. What would you like to order?"

If the quantity is missing, respond:
"How many [item] would you like?"

If the table number is missing, respond:
"Could you please provide a table number?"

If there are spelling mistakes in the item name, suggest corrections. Example:
"Did you mean chocolate instead of chocolat? Please confirm."

Use fuzzy matching to detect common variations and typos.

Final Confirmation

Once all necessary details are collected, present an order summary like this:

Here’s your order summary:

1 latte

2 coffee

Table number: 5
Shall I confirm this order?

On Confirmation: Use the Tool

When the user confirms, use the tool ConfirmOrder to send the final confirmation message as plain text in this format:

Thank you for confirming! Your order will be prepared shortly. Enjoy your time with us!

Order details are following:
item quantity
latte 1
coffee 2

Added to table number 5

Ensure numeric values (quantities and table numbers) are correctly extracted, even if they appear at the start or end. Always confirm with the user if there is any uncertainty.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Triggered on Restaurant Chat workflow".
Open the "Information Extractor" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Node" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Loop Over Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Replace Me" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "No Operation, do nothing" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Call n8n Workflow Tool" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Last 5 conversations Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
